scenarios.title: Au café
scenarios.description: Order a coffee and pastry at a Parisian café
scenarios.opening_line: Bonjour ! Bienvenue au Café de Flore. Qu'est-ce que je vous sers ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Hello! Welcome to Café de Flore. What can I get you?
tips: Use 'je voudrais' (I would like) to order politely
Say 'l'addition, s'il vous plaît' to ask for the check

scenarios.title: Au restaurant
scenarios.description: Have dinner at a French restaurant, from ordering to paying
scenarios.opening_line: Bonsoir et bienvenue chez Le Petit Bistrot. Avez-vous une réservation ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Good evening and welcome to Le Petit Bistrot. Do you have a reservation?
tips: 'Le plat du jour' is the daily special and usually a great choice
In France, you typically need to ask for the check -- it won't come automatically
Tipping is not required in France as service is included, but rounding up is appreciated

scenarios.title: Demander son chemin
scenarios.description: Ask a passerby for directions to a landmark in Paris
tips: Start with 'Excusez-moi' to get someone's attention politely
Use 'Où est...?' (Where is...?) for simple direction questions
'C'est loin d'ici ?' means 'Is it far from here?'

scenarios.title: Acheter un billet de train
scenarios.description: Purchase a train ticket at a French railway station
scenarios.opening_line_en: Hello. Where would you like to go?
tips: 'Un aller simple' is a one-way ticket, 'un aller-retour' is round trip
Ask 'À quelle heure part le prochain train ?' for the next departure time
SNCF is the French national railway company

scenarios.title: Arrivée à l'hôtel
scenarios.description: Check into a hotel, ask about amenities and breakfast
scenarios.opening_line: Bonsoir et bienvenue à l'Hôtel du Marais. Vous avez une réservation ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Good evening and welcome to the Hôtel du Marais. Do you have a reservation?
tips: 'J'ai une réservation au nom de...' means 'I have a reservation under the name...'
Ask 'Le petit-déjeuner est inclus ?' to check if breakfast is included
'À quelle heure est le petit-déjeuner ?' asks what time breakfast is served

scenarios.title: Au marché
scenarios.description: Buy fresh produce and goods at an outdoor French market
scenarios.opening_line: Bonjour ! Regardez ces belles tomates ! Qu'est-ce qui vous ferait plaisir ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Hello! Look at these beautiful tomatoes! What would you like?
tips: Use 'Je voudrais un kilo de...' to ask for a kilogram of something
'C'est combien ?' is the simplest way to ask the price
French markets typically use metric -- 'une livre' is roughly half a kilogram

scenarios.title: Dans un magasin de vêtements
scenarios.description: Browse and try on clothes at a French boutique
scenarios.opening_line: Bonjour ! Est-ce que je peux vous aider ou vous préférez regarder ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Hello! Can I help you or would you prefer to browse?
tips: 'Je fais du...' followed by a number is how you state your size
'Est-ce que je peux essayer ?' means 'Can I try this on?'
'Vous l'avez en d'autres couleurs ?' asks if they have it in other colors

scenarios.title: Se présenter
scenarios.description: Meet someone new and introduce yourself at a social gathering
scenarios.opening_line: Bonsoir ! Je ne crois pas qu'on se connaisse. Moi, c'est Marie.
scenarios.opening_line_en: Good evening! I don't think we've met. I'm Marie.
tips: 'Je m'appelle...' is 'My name is...' and 'Moi, c'est...' is more casual
'Enchanté(e)' means 'Nice to meet you' -- add the 'e' if you're female
Use 'tu' with peers and 'vous' with strangers or in formal settings

scenarios.title: Faire la conversation
scenarios.description: Have a casual conversation about weather, weekend plans, and hobbies
scenarios.opening_line: Salut ! Quel beau temps aujourd'hui, non ? Tu as des projets pour le week-end ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: Hi! Beautiful weather today, right? Do you have plans for the weekend?
tips: Weather is a universal conversation starter: 'Il fait beau' (It's nice out), 'Il pleut' (It's raining)
'J'aime bien...' is a softer way to say you like something than 'J'aime'
Use 'Et toi ?' (informal) or 'Et vous ?' (formal) to turn the question back

scenarios.title: Demander de l'aide
scenarios.description: Ask for help when you're lost, confused, or need assistance
scenarios.opening_line: Vous avez l'air perdu. Est-ce que tout va bien ?
scenarios.opening_line_en: You look lost. Is everything okay?
tips: 'Je ne parle pas bien français' (I don't speak French well) sets expectations
'Pouvez-vous parler plus lentement ?' asks someone to speak more slowly
Don't be afraid to say 'Je ne comprends pas' (I don't understand) -- people appreciate the effort
